title: Worked Solution: q_tb_circle_perimeter_area_applications_001
content: Question: Find the perimeter of a semicircular figure of radius 14 cm, including the diameter. Use pi = 22/7.. Worked solution: Perimeter of the semicircular figure = semicircular arc + diameter = pi r + 2r = 22/7 x 14 + 28 = 44 + 28 = 72 cm.. Final answer: 72 cm

concept_name: Perimeter and Area Applications on Circle Figures
title: Worked Solution: q_tb_circle_perimeter_area_applications_002
content: Question: A quadrant has radius 14 cm. Find its perimeter using pi = 22/7.. Worked solution: Perimeter of a quadrant = arc length of one-fourth of a circle + two radii = (1/4)(2pi r) + 2r = (1/2)pi r + 2r = 22 + 28 = 50 cm.. Final answer: 50 cm

title: Worked Solution: q_tb_ap_applications_002
content: Question: A child saves Rs 5 on the first day, Rs 8 on the second day, Rs 11 on the third day, and continues in the same pattern. How much will the child save in 20 days?. Worked solution: Savings form an A.P. with a = 5, d = 3, and n = 20. Then S_20 = 20/2 [2 x 5 + 19 x 3] = 10(10 + 57) = 670. So the child saves Rs 670 in 20 days.. Final answer: 670 rupees

title: Worked Solution: q_tb_ap_applications_001
content: Question: In an auditorium, the first row has 20 seats, the second row has 22 seats, and each next row has 2 more seats than the previous row. How many seats are there in the first 15 rows?. Worked solution: The number of seats forms an AP with a = 20, d = 2, and n = 15. So S_15 = 15/2 [2(20) + 14 x 2] = 15/2 (40 + 28) = 15/2 x 68 = 510.. Final answer: 510

title: Worked Solution: q_tb_ap_nth_term_001

content: Question: Check whether the points (1, 1), (2, 3), and (3, 5) are collinear.. Worked solution: Use the area test. Area = 1/2 |1(3 - 5) + 2(5 - 1) + 3(1 - 3)| = 1/2 |-2 + 8 - 6| = 0. Since the area is zero, the points are collinear.. Final answer: Yes, the points are collinear.

Substituting the points gives area = 1/2 |0(0 - 3) + 4(3 - 0) + 1(0 - 0)| = 1/2 x 12 = 6 square units.. Final answer: 6 square units

content: Question: Find the point that divides the line segment joining (1, 2) and (7, 8) internally in the ratio 1:2.. Worked solution: If P divides A(x1, y1) and B(x2, y2) in the ratio m:n, then P = ((mx2 + nx1)/(m + n), (my2 + ny1)/(m + n)). With m = 1, n = 2, A = (1, 2), and B = (7, 8), P = ((1 x 7 + 2 x 1)/3, (1 x 8 + 2 x 2)/3) = (9/3, 12/3) = (3, 4).. Final answer: (3, 4)

content: Question: Find the remainder when p(x) = x^3 - 2x^2 + 4x - 5 is divided by x - 3.. Worked solution: By the remainder theorem, the remainder on dividing by x - 3 is p(3). Compute p(3) = 27 - 18 + 12 - 5 = 16. Therefore the remainder is 16.. Final answer: 16

title: Worked Solution: q_poly_004
content: Question: Check whether x - 2 is a factor of p(x) = x^3 - 4x^2 + x + 6. If it is, write one complete factorization.. Worked solution: By the factor theorem, x - 2 is a factor if p(2) = 0. Compute p(2) = 8 - 16 + 2 + 6 = 0, so x - 2 is a factor. Dividing p(x) by x - 2 gives x^2 - 2x - 3, which factors as (x - 3)(x + 1). Therefore p(x) = (x - 2)(x - 3)(x + 1).. Final answer: Yes; p(x) = (x - 2)(x - 3)(x + 1)

content: Question: Use Euclid's division lemma to express 455 in the form 42q + r, where 0 <= r < 42. Find q and r.. Worked solution: Divide 455 by 42. title: Worked Solution: q_tb_fundamental_theorem_arithmetic_002
content: Question: Why does the product of two positive integers have a unique prime factorisation apart from the order of the prime factors?. Worked solution: The Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic states that every composite number can be expressed as a product of primes, and this factorisation is unique except for the order of the primes. This is why prime factorisation gives consistent HCF and LCM results.. Final answer: Because every composite number can be broken into primes uniquely up to order.

content: Question: Use prime factorisation to find the HCF and LCM of 72 and 120.. Worked solution: Prime factorise: 72 = 2^3 x 3^2 and 120 = 2^3 x 3 x 5. The HCF is the product of the smallest powers of common primes: 2^3 x 3 = 24. The LCM is the product of the greatest powers of all involved primes: 2^3 x 3^2 x 5 = 360.. Final answer: HCF = 24, LCM = 360

title: Worked Solution: q_rn_004
content: Question: Show that 5 + sqrt(3) is irrational.. Worked solution: Assume 5 + sqrt(3) is rational. Then subtracting 5, which is rational, would imply sqrt(3) is rational. title: Worked Solution: q_tri_005
content: Question: In triangle ABC, D lies on AB and E lies on AC such that DE is parallel to BC. If AD = 3 cm, DB = x cm, AE = 4 cm, and EC = x + 2 cm, find x.. Worked solution: Since DE || BC, the intercepted sides are proportional, so AD/DB = AE/EC. Substitute the given values: 3/x = 4/(x + 2). Cross-multiplying gives 3x + 6 = 4x, hence x = 6.. Final answer: 6

Worked solution: For similar triangles, the ratio of areas equals the square of the ratio of corresponding sides. So smaller:larger area = 3^2:5^2 = 9:25. Worked solution: Let the height of the tower be h. Using tan 60 degrees = h/20 and tan 60 degrees = sqrt(3), we get h = 20*sqrt(3) m, which is approximately 34.64 m.. Worked solution: Compute tan(theta) = sin(theta)/cos(theta) = (3/5)/(4/5) = 3/4. Then 1 + tan^2(theta) = 1 + 9/16 = 25/16. Also sec(theta) = 1/cos(theta) = 5/4, so sec^2(theta) = 25/16. Hence the identity is verified.. Final answer: Both sides equal 25/16
